title: Analysis plan
text: Estimate associations between maternal prenatal diet exposures and child metabolite outcomes across visit 4 (6 months), visit 5 (12 months), visit 6 (24 months), and visit 7 (5-6 years).

title: Analysis plan
text: Estimate associations between maternal prenatal diet exposures and child metabolite outcomes across visit 4 (6 months), visit 5 (12 months), visit 6 (24 months), and visit 7 (5-6 years).

title: Analysis plan
text:
2 Scope

Primary exposure family: maternal prenatal diet metrics.
Primary outcomes: metabolite-level outcomes.
Secondary outcomes: pathway or cluster scores.

title: Analysis plan
text:
3 Data processing steps

Build visit-specific analysis panels (visit 4, 5, 6, 7) using metabolite availability and linked child IDs.
Confirm exposure coding and units.
Log-transform skewed metabolite variables as needed.
Standardize outcomes within visit/timepoint for comparability.
Produce missingness table for all candidate covariates.
Run covariate balance tests across intervention groups within each visit panel (Kruskal-Wallis for continuous, Fisher exact for discrete covariates including ordinal and nominal categorical) and apply BH correction.

title: Analysis plan
text:
4 Covariate strategy
Primary covariate block:

Intervention, CGender, MEthnicity, MDise

Sensitivity covariate block:

MprepBMI, MAge1, MprevGDM_new, Bfdurationm, MPRSmoke, MUniEdu, CWeightBirth

Rule: keep primary estimates based on primary block; report sensitivity shifts separately. Age-specific, mutual-timing, and longitudinal models additionally adjust for visit-specific fasting time when available as a measurement-condition covariate.
Covariates are pre-specified. Test results are reported and are not used as the sole inclusion rule.

title: Analysis plan
text:
5 Model plan

Age-specific model:

lm(Y_age ~ E + primary_covariates + fasting_age)

Mutual timing model:

lm(Y_age ~ E_early + E_late + primary_covariates + fasting_age)

Longitudinal mixed model:

lmer(Y_it ~ E + age_t + E:age_t + primary_covariates + fasting_it + (1|child))

Sensitivity model:

add sensitivity block to models 1-3 and compare coefficients.

title: Analysis plan
text:
6 Multiplicity

Apply Benjamini-Hochberg correction across metabolites, with the adjustment scope defined by model family.
Age-specific outputs apply BH within each visit-specific exposure model.
Mutual timing outputs apply BH separately to the early-term and late-term coefficients within each visit-specific exposure pair.
Longitudinal outputs apply BH separately within each exposure and coefficient family.
Report raw p, adjusted q, effect size, and confidence interval.

title: Analysis plan
text:
7 Deliverables

Main-effect table by age.
Mutual timing table (early and late windows together).
Longitudinal interaction table (E:age).
Sensitivity comparison table.
Plots: volcano/effect plot and trajectory plot.

title: Analysis plan
text:
8 Tools

Data: haven, readxl, dplyr, tidyr
Models: stats, lmerTest, broom, broom.mixed
Figures: ggplot2, patchwork
DAG and adjustment checks: dagitty

title: Data summary
text:
Show code
source("analysis_data_utils.R")
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

has_venn <- requireNamespace("ggVennDiagram", quietly = TRUE)

analysis_obj <- prepare_analysis_data(
  mae_path = "../data/MAE_original.rds",
  visits = paste0("visit_", 4:7)
)

df <- analysis_obj$data
metabolite_cols <- analysis_obj$metabolite_cols
cluster_map <- analysis_obj$cluster_map
raw_df <- extract_long_from_mae(
  analysis_obj$mae,
  visits = paste0("visit_", 4:7)
)

first_non_missing <- function(x) {
  idx <- which(!is.na(x))
  if (length(idx) == 0) return(NA)
  x[[idx[1]]]
}

visit_label <- function(v) dplyr::case_when(
  v == 4L ~ "6 Months",
  v == 5L ~ "12 Months",
  v == 6L ~ "24 Months",
  v == 7L ~ "5-6 Years",
  TRUE ~ paste("Visit", v)
)

pretty_var <- function(x) {
  x <- sub("^M", "", x)
  x <- gsub("DietaryPatterns", "Dietary Patterns ", x)
  x <- gsub("DIINormalDiet", "Dietary Inflammatory Index ", x)
  x <- gsub("DIIDensityDiet", "Dietary Inflammatory Index Density ", x)
  x <- gsub("^IDQ1$", "IDQ", x)
  x <- gsub("^IDQ2$", "IDQ", x)
  x <- gsub("EPercent", "Percent ", x)
  x <- gsub("Fibtot", "Fibre ", x)
  x <- gsub("CHO", "Carbohydrate", x)
  x <- gsub("Pro", "Protein", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn3", "n-3 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn6", "n-6 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("SFA", "SFA", x)
  x <- gsub("MUFA", "MUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("PUFA", "PUFA", x)
  x <- sub("[12]$", "", x)
  x <- gsub("_", " ", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("\\s+", " ", trimws(x))
  tools::toTitleCase(tolower(x))
}

title: Data summary
text:
2 Sample coverage


Show code
coverage_tbl <- raw_df %>%
  distinct(StudyID, visit_num) %>%
  count(visit_num, name = "n_children") %>%
  mutate(child_age = visit_label(visit_num)) %>%
  select(child_age, n_children)

make_table(coverage_tbl, caption = "Children contributing metabolomics panels by child age")

title: Data summary
text:
3 Maternal baseline characteristics


Show code
maternal_vars <- c(
  "Intervention", "CGender", "MEthnicity", "MDise",
  "MprepBMI", "MAge1", "MprevGDM_new", "Bfdurationm",
  "MPRSmoke", "MUniEdu", "CWeightBirth"
)
maternal_vars <- maternal_vars[maternal_vars %in% names(df)]

maternal_df <- df %>%
  arrange(StudyID, visit_num) %>%
  group_by(StudyID) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(maternal_vars), first_non_missing), .groups = "drop")

if (length(maternal_vars) == 0) {
  maternal_summary_tbl <- data.frame(message = "Maternal baseline summary unavailable")
} else {
  maternal_summary_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(maternal_vars, function(var_nm) {
    x <- maternal_df[[var_nm]]
    x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(x)))
    is_continuous <- sum(is.finite(x_num)) >= 20L && length(unique(x_num[is.finite(x_num)])) >= 8L

    if (is_continuous) {
      data.frame(
        variable = pretty_var(var_nm),
        variable_code = var_nm,
        type = "continuous",
        non_missing = sum(!is.na(x_num)),
        summary = paste0(
          round(mean(x_num, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
          " (sd ", round(sd(x_num, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "); median ",
          round(stats::median(x_num, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
          " [", round(stats::quantile(x_num, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
          ", ", round(stats::quantile(x_num, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "]"
        ),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    } else {
      lev <- sort(table(as.character(x)), decreasing = TRUE)
      top_level <- if (length(lev) == 0) NA_character_ else names(lev)[1]
      top_n <- if (length(lev) == 0) 0 else unname(lev[1])
      data.frame(
        variable = pretty_var(var_nm),
        variable_code = var_nm,
        type = "categorical",
        non_missing = sum(!is.na(x)),
        summary = paste0("most common: ", top_level, " (", top_n, ")"),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }))
}

make_table(maternal_summary_tbl, caption = "Maternal baseline characteristics")

title: Data summary
text:
4 Visit overlap


Show code
id_sets <- split(df$StudyID, df$visit_num)
id_sets <- lapply(id_sets, function(x) sort(unique(as.character(x))))
id_sets <- id_sets[order(as.integer(names(id_sets)))]
names(id_sets) <- vapply(as.integer(names(id_sets)), visit_label, character(1))

if (!has_venn || length(id_sets) < 2) {
  ggplot() +
    annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "Visit-overlap plot unavailable") +
    theme_void()
} else {
  ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(id_sets, label = "both") +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu") +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = 0.18)) +
    ggplot2::theme_void(base_size = 12) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 28)
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = "Child overlap across ages",
      fill = "Count"
    )
}

title: Data summary
text:
5 Exposure coverage


Show code
exposure_vars <- c(
  "MDietaryPatterns_1", "MDIINormalDiet1", "MDIIDensityDiet1", "MIDQ1",
  "MEPercentProDiet1", "MEPercentCHODiet1", "MEPercentFatDiet1",
  "MEPercentPUFADiet1", "MEPercentMUFADiet1", "MEPercentSFADiet1",
  "MEPercentFAn3Diet1", "MEPercentFAn6Diet1", "MEPercentFibtotDiet1",
  "MDietaryPatterns_2", "MDIINormalDiet2", "MDIIDensityDiet2", "MIDQ2",
  "MEPercentProDiet2", "MEPercentCHODiet2", "MEPercentFatDiet2",
  "MEPercentPUFADiet2", "MEPercentMUFADiet2", "MEPercentSFADiet2",
  "MEPercentFAn3Diet2", "MEPercentFAn6Diet2", "MEPercentFibtotDiet2"
)

prenatal_df <- df %>%
  arrange(StudyID, visit_num) %>%
  group_by(StudyID) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(exposure_vars), first_non_missing), .groups = "drop")

exposure_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(c("1", "2"), function(w) {
  current_exposures <- exposure_vars[grepl(paste0(w, "$"), exposure_vars)]
  window_lab <- if (w == "1") "Early Pregnancy" else "Late Pregnancy"

  bind_rows(lapply(current_exposures, function(var_nm) {
    x <- prenatal_df[[var_nm]]
    x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(x)))
    is_continuous <- sum(is.finite(x_num)) >= 20L && length(unique(x_num[is.finite(x_num)])) >= 8L

    if (is_continuous) {
      data.frame(
        pregnancy_window = window_lab,
        exposure = pretty_var(var_nm),
        variable = var_nm,
        type = "continuous",
        non_missing = sum(!is.na(x_num)),
        summary = paste0(
          round(mean(x_num, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
          " (sd ", round(sd(x_num, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "); median ",
          round(stats::median(x_num, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        ),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    } else {
      lev <- sort(table(as.character(x)), decreasing = TRUE)
      top_level <- names(lev)[1]
      top_n <- if (length(lev) == 0) 0 else unname(lev[1])
      data.frame(
        pregnancy_window = window_lab,
        exposure = pretty_var(var_nm),
        variable = var_nm,
        type = "categorical",
        non_missing = sum(!is.na(x)),
        summary = paste0("most common: ", top_level, " (", top_n, ")"),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }))
}))

make_table(exposure_tbl, caption = "Maternal prenatal exposure distributions by pregnancy window")

title: Data summary
text:
6 Metabolite coverage


Show code
metabolite_missing <- bind_rows(lapply(sort(unique(df$visit_num)), function(v) {
  dv <- df %>% filter(visit_num == v)
  miss <- colMeans(is.na(dv[, metabolite_cols, drop = FALSE]))

  data.frame(
    child_age = visit_label(v),
    metabolites = length(metabolite_cols),
    median_missing_fraction = median(miss),
    p90_missing_fraction = as.numeric(quantile(miss, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
    max_missing_fraction = max(miss),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

make_table(round_numeric(metabolite_missing, 3), caption = "Metabolite missingness by child age")

title: Data summary
text:
7 Global structure (PCA) with top loadings


Show code
pca_details <- compute_pca_details(
  df = df,
  feature_cols = metabolite_cols,
  group_cols = c("visit"),
  min_feature_obs = 0.7,
  top_n_loadings = 5L
)

if (is.null(pca_details)) {
  ggplot() +
    annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "PCA unavailable: insufficient complete feature matrix") +
    theme_void()
} else {
  scores <- pca_details$scores
  scores$visit <- factor(gsub("_", " ", scores$visit), levels = sort(unique(gsub("_", " ", scores$visit))))
  visit_counts <- scores %>%
    count(visit, name = "n") %>%
    mutate(label = paste0(visit, " (n=", n, ")"))
  label_map <- setNames(visit_counts$label, as.character(visit_counts$visit))
  legend_labels <- unname(label_map[levels(scores$visit)])
  top_loadings_plot <- pca_details$top_loadings
  score_scale <- max(abs(scores$PC1), abs(scores$PC2), na.rm = TRUE)

  has_ellipse <- scores %>%
    count(visit, name = "n") %>%
    summarise(ok = all(n >= 3) & n() >= 2) %>%
    pull(ok)

  p <- ggplot(scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = visit)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.7) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    coord_equal() +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", labels = legend_labels, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = "PCA across child ages",
      x = paste0("PC1 (", round(100 * pca_details$var_explained[1], 1), "%)"),
      y = paste0("PC2 (", round(100 * pca_details$var_explained[2], 1), "%)"),
      color = "Visit"
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  if (isTRUE(has_ellipse)) {
    p <- p + stat_ellipse(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.7)
  }

  p +
    geom_segment(
      data = top_loadings_plot,
      aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1_scaled, yend = PC2_scaled),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.45,
      arrow = arrow(length = grid::unit(0.18, "cm"))
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = top_loadings_plot,
      aes(x = PC1_scaled, y = PC2_scaled, label = feature),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      size = 2.8,
      color = "black",
      check_overlap = TRUE,
      nudge_y = 0.02 * score_scale
    )
}











Show code
if (is.null(pca_details)) {
  loading_tbl <- data.frame(message = "Top loadings unavailable: insufficient complete feature matrix")
} else {
  loading_tbl <- pca_details$loadings %>%
    slice_head(n = 15)
}

make_table(round_numeric(loading_tbl, 3), caption = "Top PCA loadings (PC1+PC2)")










Show code
if (!is.null(pca_details)) {
  loading_plot_tbl <- pca_details$loadings %>%
    select(feature, PC1, PC2) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(PC1, PC2), names_to = "component", values_to = "loading") %>%
    group_by(component) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = abs(loading), n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      effect = ifelse(loading >= 0, "positive", "negative"),
      feature = reorder(feature, abs(loading))
    )

  ggplot(loading_plot_tbl, aes(x = abs(loading), y = feature, fill = effect)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.85) +
    facet_wrap(~component, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(negative = "#4B00FF", positive = "#FF1A1A")) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    labs(
      title = "Top PCA loadings by component",
      x = "Value",
      y = "Feature",
      fill = "Effect"
    )
}

title: Covariate and model specification
text:
3 Covariate specification

3.1 Covariate selection framework
Covariate selection is pre-specified and DAG-informed:

The primary adjustment set is fixed in advance.
Balance tests and missingness summaries are reported.
Secondary models add sensitivity covariates.



Show code
graph LR
    A[Covariate Candidate] --> B{In Core DAG?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Status: keep_core]
    B -- No --> D{In Sensitivity Block?}
    D -- Yes --> E{Screening Signal?<br>p < 0.20 OR Beta Change > 10%}
    E -- Yes --> F[Status: keep_sensitivity]
    E -- No --> G[Status: consider_drop]
    D -- No --> G





graph LR
    A[Covariate Candidate] --> B{In Core DAG?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Status: keep_core]
    B -- No --> D{In Sensitivity Block?}
    D -- Yes --> E{Screening Signal?<br>p < 0.20 OR Beta Change > 10%}
    E -- Yes --> F[Status: keep_sensitivity]
    E -- No --> G[Status: consider_drop]
    D -- No --> G








3.2 Group balance tests across intervention arms






Why this matters: Balance Diagnostics



These tests check whether covariates are balanced across the intervention arms. Because this study evaluates a randomized trial context, minor imbalances can arise by chance. Note that standard guidelines discourage using p-values alone to drop covariates—pre-specified confounders are retained regardless of balance test significance.


Covariate balance diagnostics across intervention groups:

Kruskal-Wallis for continuous covariates.
Fisher exact for discrete covariates (ordinal and nominal categorical).
BH adjusted p-values across all visits in the combined table.

These p-values are not used alone to choose adjustment covariates. The intervention variable itself is the grouping variable and is therefore not included as a balance-test covariate.


Show code
run_covariate_test <- function(df, group_var, covariate, test_method) {
  out <- data.frame(
    var = covariate,
    test = test_method,
    p_value = NA_real_,
    p_adj_bh = NA_real_,
    n_groups = NA_integer_,
    note = NA_character_,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  if (!covariate %in% names(df)) {
    out$note <- "covariate_missing_in_data"
    return(out)
  }
  if (!group_var %in% names(df)) {
    out$note <- "group_var_missing_in_data"
    return(out)
  }
  if (!test_method %in% c("anova", "kruskal_wallis", "fisher_or_chisq")) {
    out$note <- "invalid_test_method"
    return(out)
  }

  x <- df[[covariate]]
  g <- df[[group_var]]
  keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(g)
  if (!any(keep)) {
    out$note <- "no_complete_cases"
    return(out)
  }

  x <- x[keep]
  g <- as.factor(g[keep])
  out$n_groups <- nlevels(g)

  if (out$n_groups < 2L) {
    out$note <- "fewer_than_two_groups"
    return(out)
  }
  if (length(unique(x)) < 2L) {
    out$note <- "constant_covariate"
    return(out)
  }

  if (identical(test_method, "anova")) {
    out$p_value <- tryCatch({
      fit <- aov(as.numeric(x) ~ g)
      summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
  } else if (identical(test_method, "kruskal_wallis")) {
    out$test <- "kruskal_wallis"
    y <- if (is.character(x) || is.factor(x)) as.numeric(factor(x)) else as.numeric(x)
    out$p_value <- tryCatch(kruskal.test(y ~ g)$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
  } else {
    x_cat <- as.factor(x)
    if (nlevels(x_cat) < 2L) {
      out$note <- "fewer_than_two_levels"
      return(out)
    }

    tbl <- table(g, x_cat)
    if (any(dim(tbl) < 2L)) {
      out$note <- "invalid_contingency_table"
      return(out)
    }

    out$p_value <- tryCatch({
      out$test <- "fisher_exact"
      fisher.test(tbl)$p.value
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
  }

  out$note <- ifelse(is.na(out$p_value), "test_failed", "ok")
  out
}

run_group_balance_panel <- function(df, group_var, covariates, panel_name) {
  res <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(nrow(covariates)), function(i) {
    run_covariate_test(
      df = df,
      group_var = group_var,
      covariate = covariates$var[i],
      test_method = covariates$test[i]
    )
  }))
  res$panel <- panel_name
  res %>% arrange(p_value)
}

plot_balance_var <- function(df, group_var, var_name, test_name) {
  x <- df[[var_name]]
  g <- as.factor(df[[group_var]])
  keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(g)

  if (!any(keep)) return(NULL)

  dat <- data.frame(
    group = as.factor(g[keep]),
    value = x[keep],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  if (test_name %in% c("anova", "kruskal_wallis")) {
    dat$value <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dat$value))
    ggplot(dat, aes(x = group, y = value)) +
      geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.4, width = 0.6) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.35, size = 1.2) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(var_name, " (", pretty_test(test_name), ")"),
        x = "Intervention",
        y = var_name
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
  } else {
    dat$value <- as.factor(dat$value)
    ggplot(dat, aes(x = group, fill = value)) +
      geom_bar(position = "fill") +
      labs(
        title = paste0(var_name, " (", pretty_test(test_name), ")"),
        x = "Intervention",
        y = "Proportion",
        fill = var_name
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
  }
}

  tbl %>%

}

  data.frame(

3.3 Symbol key

\(E\): exposure
\(Y\): outcome

3.4 Causal roles

Confounder: common cause of E and Y; adjust in primary models.
Mediator: on path E -> mediator -> Y; do not include in primary confounder set.
Collider: common effect of two causes; do not condition on it.

3.7 Collinearity






Understanding Collinearity & VIF




Pairwise Screen: A Spearman correlation, Epsilon-squared, or Cramer’s V \(\ge 0.70\) highlights redundant covariate pairs. In such cases, standard errors of the exposure effect estimate may inflate if both are kept.
Model-Matrix VIF: Variance Inflation Factors (VIF) summarize design-wide multicollinearity. Values below 5 indicate no concern. Values above 5 (or rank deficiency indicating exact aliasing) require reviewing the models for redundant parameters or category sparseness.



Collinearity is checked before interpreting expanded adjustment sets:

Numeric covariate pairs are screened with Spearman correlations within each visit-linked panel.
Categorical covariate pairs are screened with Cramer’s V.
Mixed continuous-categorical pairs are screened with Epsilon-squared from Kruskal-Wallis tests.
Pairs with absolute values at or above 0.70 are flagged for review.



Show code
cramers_v <- function(x, y) {
  keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(y)
  x <- as.factor(x[keep])
  y <- as.factor(y[keep])
  if (length(x) == 0 || nlevels(x) < 2L || nlevels(y) < 2L) return(NA_real_)
  tab <- table(x, y)
  chi <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, correct = FALSE))
  n <- sum(tab)
  denom <- n * (min(dim(tab)) - 1)
  if (!is.finite(denom) || denom <= 0) return(NA_real_)
  sqrt(as.numeric(chi$statistic) / denom)
}

kruskal_epsilon_sq <- function(statistic, n, groups) {
  if (!is.finite(statistic) || !is.finite(n) || !is.finite(groups) || n <= groups) return(NA_real_)
  max(0, as.numeric((statistic - groups + 1) / (n - groups)))
}

collinearity_screen <- function(df, vars, panel_name, threshold = 0.70) {
  vars <- intersect(vars, names(df))
  vars <- vars[vapply(vars, function(v) sum(!is.na(df[[v]])) >= 10, logical(1))]
  if (length(vars) < 2L) return(data.frame())

  is_continuous <- function(val) {
    if (is.character(val) || is.factor(val) || is.logical(val) || inherits(val, "haven_labelled")) return(FALSE)
    val_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(val))
    sum(is.finite(val_num)) >= 8 && length(unique(val_num[is.finite(val_num)])) >= 6
  }

  pairs <- utils::combn(vars, 2, simplify = FALSE)
  bind_rows(lapply(pairs, function(pair) {
    x <- df[[pair[[1]]]]
    y <- df[[pair[[2]]]]
    
    keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(y)
    n_complete <- sum(keep)
    if (n_complete < 10) return(NULL)
    
    x_cont <- is_continuous(x)
    y_cont <- is_continuous(y)
    
    if (x_cont && y_cont) {
      value <- suppressWarnings(cor(as.numeric(x), as.numeric(y), use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman"))
      metric <- "spearman_rho"
      flag <- is.finite(value) && abs(value) >= threshold
    } else if (!x_cont && !y_cont) {
      value <- cramers_v(x, y)
      metric <- "cramers_v"
      flag <- is.finite(value) && value >= threshold
    } else {
      # Mixed continuous-categorical pair
      if (x_cont) {
        continuous_val <- as.numeric(x)
        categorical_val <- factor(as.character(y))
      } else {
        continuous_val <- as.numeric(y)
        categorical_val <- factor(as.character(x))
      }
      
      if (nlevels(categorical_val) < 2) return(NULL)
      tst <- suppressWarnings(kruskal.test(continuous_val ~ categorical_val))
      value <- kruskal_epsilon_sq(tst$statistic, n_complete, nlevels(categorical_val))
      metric <- "epsilon_sq"
      flag <- is.finite(value) && value >= threshold
    }

    data.frame(
      panel = panel_name,
      covariate_1 = pair[[1]],
      covariate_2 = pair[[2]],
      metric = metric,
      value = value,
      flagged = flag,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })) %>%
    filter(is.finite(value)) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(value)))
}

collinearity_vars <- unique(c(core_covars_husna, sensitivity_covars_husna, paste0("CFastingHoursBloodSample", visit_levels_husna)))

collinearity_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(names(clinical_visit_map_husna), function(vn) {
  collinearity_screen(clinical_visit_map_husna[[vn]], collinearity_vars, vn)
})) %>%

3.8 Model-matrix collinearity
Model-matrix collinearity is checked before interpreting expanded adjustment sets. For each visit-linked panel, the fixed-effect design matrix from expanded covariates is evaluated. Max VIF summarizes the maximum column-level VIF after removing the intercept. Rank-deficient indicates exact aliasing in the design matrix.


Show code
safe_vif_max <- function(X) {
  if (!is.matrix(X) || nrow(X) < 5 || ncol(X) < 2) return(NA_real_)
  keep <- apply(X, 2, function(col) stats::sd(col, na.rm = TRUE) > 0)
  X <- X[, keep, drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(X) < 2) return(NA_real_)

  vifs <- sapply(seq_len(ncol(X)), function(j) {
    y <- X[, j]
    Z <- X[, -j, drop = FALSE]
    fit <- tryCatch(lm(y ~ Z), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(fit)) return(NA_real_)
    r2 <- summary(fit)$r.squared
    if (!is.finite(r2) || r2 >= 1) return(Inf)
    1 / (1 - r2)
  })
  suppressWarnings(max(vifs, na.rm = TRUE))
}

design_collinearity <- bind_rows(lapply(names(clinical_visit_map_husna), function(vn) {
  df <- clinical_visit_map_husna[[vn]]
  vars_here <- unique(c(core_covars_husna, sensitivity_covars_husna, paste0("CFastingHoursBloodSample", gsub("visit", "", vn))))
  vars_here <- vars_here[vars_here %in% names(df)]
  if (length(vars_here) == 0L) {
    return(data.frame(
      panel = gsub("visit", "visit ", vn),
      n_complete = 0L,
      n_columns = 0L,
      rank = 0L,
      rank_deficient = NA,
      max_vif = NA_real_,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }

  dat <- df[, vars_here, drop = FALSE]
  dat <- dat[stats::complete.cases(dat), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(dat) < 10L) {
    return(data.frame(
      panel = gsub("visit", "visit ", vn),
      n_complete = nrow(dat),
      n_columns = 0L,
      rank = 0L,
      rank_deficient = NA,
      max_vif = NA_real_,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }

  form <- as.formula(paste("~", paste(vars_here, collapse = " + ")))
  X <- model.matrix(form, data = dat)
  rank_x <- qr(X)$rank
  ncol_x <- ncol(X)
  X_no_intercept <- X[, colnames(X) != "(Intercept)", drop = FALSE]

  data.frame(
    panel = gsub("visit", "visit ", vn),
    n_complete = nrow(dat),
    n_columns = ncol_x,
    rank = rank_x,
    rank_deficient = rank_x < ncol_x,
    max_vif = safe_vif_max(X_no_intercept),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

design_collinearity %>%
  mutate(
    max_vif = ifelse(is.finite(max_vif), round(max_vif, 2), Inf)
  ) %>%
  show_table(
    page_len = 10,
    title = "Model-matrix collinearity summary",
    caption = "Model-matrix collinearity summary"
  )


Model-matrix collinearity summary






3.9 Exposure-covariate screening






Purposeful Selection Screening



Purposeful-style screening checks whether candidate variables show empirical associations with the exposures and/or outcomes, which can support sensitivity-adjustment decisions but does not establish confounding on its own. * A significance threshold of \(p < 0.20\) is typically used to screen potential candidates rather than the standard \(p < 0.05\) to avoid omitting weak confounders. * Small \(q\)-values (BH adjusted) indicate associations that survive multiple-testing adjustments.


This table screens covariate associations with maternal diet exposures across visit-linked clinical panels.
Tests by variable type:

Spearman for continuous exposure and continuous covariate pairs.
Kruskal-Wallis for continuous exposure vs categorical covariate, and categorical exposure vs continuous covariate.
Fisher exact for categorical exposure and categorical covariate pairs.

Tests is the number of exposure-covariate tests in the row. Min p is the smallest raw p-value across those tests. Min q is the smallest BH-adjusted p-value within visit panel. q < 0.10 is the number of exposure-covariate tests with q < 0.10.


Show code
exposures_early_husna <- c(
  "MDietaryPatterns_1", "MDIINormalDiet1", "MDIIDensityDiet1", "MIDQ1",
  "MEPercentProDiet1", "MEPercentCHODiet1", "MEPercentFatDiet1", "MEPercentPUFADiet1",
  "MEPercentMUFADiet1", "MEPercentSFADiet1", "MEPercentFAn3Diet1", "MEPercentFAn6Diet1", "MEPercentFibtotDiet1"
)
exposures_late_husna <- c(
  "MDietaryPatterns_2", "MDIINormalDiet2", "MDIIDensityDiet2", "MIDQ2",
  "MEPercentProDiet2", "MEPercentCHODiet2", "MEPercentFatDiet2", "MEPercentPUFADiet2",
  "MEPercentMUFADiet2", "MEPercentSFADiet2", "MEPercentFAn3Diet2", "MEPercentFAn6Diet2", "MEPercentFibtotDiet2"
)
exposure_candidates_husna <- unique(c(exposures_early_husna, exposures_late_husna))

categorical_vars_husna <- c(
  "Intervention", "CGender", "MEthnicity", "MDise",
  "MprevGDM_new", "MPRSmoke", "MUniEdu"
)

var_kind_husna <- function(x, nm = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(nm) && nm %in% categorical_vars_husna) return("categorical")
  if (is.character(x) || is.factor(x)) return("categorical")
  x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
  if (sum(is.finite(x_num)) < 8) return("categorical")
  if (length(unique(x_num[is.finite(x_num)])) < 6) return("categorical")
  "continuous"
}

assoc_test_husna <- function(dat, exposure, covar, panel_name) {
  if (!all(c(exposure, covar) %in% names(dat))) {
    return(tibble(panel = panel_name, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "missing", n = 0L, p.value = NA_real_))
  }
  dd <- dat[, c(exposure, covar), drop = FALSE]
  dd <- dd[stats::complete.cases(dd), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(dd) < 20) {
    return(tibble(panel = panel_name, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "too_few_complete", n = nrow(dd), p.value = NA_real_))
  }

  x_kind <- var_kind_husna(dd[[exposure]], exposure)
  z_kind <- var_kind_husna(dd[[covar]], covar)
  x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dd[[exposure]]))
  z_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dd[[covar]]))

  if (x_kind == "continuous" && z_kind == "continuous") {
    tst <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x_num, z_num, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
    return(tibble(panel = panel_name, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "Spearman", n = nrow(dd), p.value = tst$p.value))
  }
  if (x_kind == "continuous" && z_kind == "categorical") {
    g <- factor(as.character(dd[[covar]]))
    if (nlevels(g) < 2) return(tibble(panel = panel_name, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "constant", n = nrow(dd), p.value = NA_real_))
    tst <- suppressWarnings(kruskal.test(x_num ~ g))
    return(tibble(panel = panel_name, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "Kruskal-Wallis", n = nrow(dd), p.value = tst$p.value))
  }
  if (x_kind == "categorical" && z_kind == "continuous") {
    g <- factor(as.character(dd[[exposure]]))
    if (nlevels(g) < 2) return(tibble(panel = panel_name, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "constant", n = nrow(dd), p.value = NA_real_))
    tst <- suppressWarnings(kruskal.test(z_num ~ g))
    return(tibble(panel = panel_name, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "Kruskal-Wallis", n = nrow(dd), p.value = tst$p.value))
  }

  tab <- table(factor(as.character(dd[[exposure]])), factor(as.character(dd[[covar]])))
  if (any(dim(tab) < 2)) return(tibble(panel = panel_name, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "constant", n = nrow(dd), p.value = NA_real_))
  tst <- suppressWarnings(fisher.test(tab))
  tibble(panel = panel_name, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "Fisher exact", n = nrow(dd), p.value = tst$p.value)
}

screen_rows_husna <- bind_rows(lapply(names(clinical_visit_map_husna), function(vn) {
  df_panel <- clinical_visit_map_husna[[vn]]
  covars_here <- unique(c(
    core_covars_husna,
    sensitivity_covars_husna,
    paste0("CFastingHoursBloodSample", gsub("visit", "", vn))
  ))
  covars_here <- covars_here[covars_here %in% names(df_panel)]
  exposures_here <- exposure_candidates_husna[exposure_candidates_husna %in% names(df_panel)]
  if (length(covars_here) == 0 || length(exposures_here) == 0) return(NULL)

  bind_rows(lapply(exposures_here, function(e) {
    bind_rows(lapply(covars_here, function(cov) assoc_test_husna(df_panel, e, cov, vn)))
  }))
})) %>%
  group_by(panel) %>%
  mutate(q.value = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH")) %>%
  ungroup()

    q_lt_0_10 = sum(q.value < 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),

3.10 Covariate-outcome screening
This table screens covariate associations with metabolite outcome structure. PCA is run on visit-specific metabolite matrices from the SPSS sheet, and each covariate is tested against the first five metabolite PCs within each visit panel.

Continuous covariates are screened with Spearman correlation against each PC.
Categorical covariates are screened with Kruskal-Wallis tests against each PC.
BH correction is applied within visit panel.



Show code
make_pc_scores_husna <- function(dat, feature_cols, prefix) {
  feature_cols <- intersect(feature_cols, names(dat))
  if (length(feature_cols) == 0) return(dat %>% select(StudyID))
  mat <- as.matrix(dat[, feature_cols, drop = FALSE])
  mat <- apply(mat, 2, function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x)))
  if (is.null(dim(mat))) mat <- matrix(mat, ncol = 1L)
  keep <- colMeans(!is.na(mat)) >= 0.7 & apply(mat, 2, stats::sd, na.rm = TRUE) > 0
  mat <- mat[, keep, drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(mat) == 0) return(dat %>% select(StudyID))
  for (j in seq_len(ncol(mat))) {
    mat[is.na(mat[, j]), j] <- stats::median(mat[, j], na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  pcs <- stats::prcomp(mat, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
  scores <- as.data.frame(pcs$x[, seq_len(min(5L, ncol(pcs$x))), drop = FALSE])
  names(scores) <- paste0(prefix, "_PC", seq_along(scores))
  bind_cols(dat %>% select(StudyID), scores)
}

pc_assoc_husna <- function(dat, covar, pc, panel_name) {
  if (!all(c(covar, pc) %in% names(dat))) {
    return(tibble(panel = panel_name, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "missing", n = 0L, p.value = NA_real_))
  }
  dd <- dat[, c(covar, pc), drop = FALSE]
  dd <- dd[stats::complete.cases(dd), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(dd) < 20) {
    return(tibble(panel = panel_name, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "too_few_complete", n = nrow(dd), p.value = NA_real_))
  }

  y <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dd[[pc]]))
  if (var_kind_husna(dd[[covar]], covar) == "continuous") {
    x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dd[[covar]]))
    tst <- suppressWarnings(stats::cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
    return(tibble(panel = panel_name, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "Spearman", n = nrow(dd), p.value = tst$p.value))
  }
  g <- factor(as.character(dd[[covar]]))
  if (nlevels(g) < 2) {
    return(tibble(panel = panel_name, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "constant", n = nrow(dd), p.value = NA_real_))
  }
  tst <- suppressWarnings(stats::kruskal.test(y ~ g))
  tibble(panel = panel_name, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "Kruskal-Wallis", n = nrow(dd), p.value = tst$p.value)
}

3.12 Covariate decision support (reference models)
Reference-model beta-change and complete-case impact are shown as practical support for retain/drop decisions.


Show code
pc1_map_husna <- bind_rows(lapply(names(clinical_visit_map_husna), function(vn) {
  vnum <- as.integer(gsub("visit", "", vn))
  d_met <- metabolite_spss %>%
    filter(visit_num == vnum) %>%
    select(any_of(c("StudyID", metabolite_cols_husna))) %>%
    mutate(StudyID = as.character(StudyID))
  d_cli <- clinical_visit_map_husna[[vn]] %>% mutate(StudyID = as.character(StudyID))
  d_panel <- left_join(d_cli, d_met, by = "StudyID")
  pc_tbl <- make_pc_scores_husna(d_panel, metabolite_cols_husna, paste0("v", vnum))
  pc_name <- names(pc_tbl)[grepl("_PC1$", names(pc_tbl))]
  if (length(pc_name) == 0) return(NULL)
  out <- left_join(d_cli, pc_tbl[, c("StudyID", pc_name[1]), drop = FALSE], by = "StudyID")
  names(out)[names(out) == pc_name[1]] <- "Y_ref"
  out$panel <- gsub("visit", "visit ", vn)
  out
}))

beta_change_ref_husna <- function(df, exposure, covars, candidate) {
  need <- unique(c("Y_ref", exposure, covars))
  need <- need[need %in% names(df)]
  if (!all(c("Y_ref", exposure, candidate) %in% need)) return(c(NA_real_, NA_integer_, NA_integer_))
  d <- df[, need, drop = FALSE]
  d <- d[stats::complete.cases(d), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(d) < 30) return(c(NA_real_, nrow(d), NA_integer_))
  if (!candidate %in% covars || !candidate %in% names(d)) return(c(NA_real_, nrow(d), NA_integer_))

  cov_full <- covars[covars %in% names(d)]
  cov_red <- setdiff(cov_full, candidate)
  f_full <- as.formula(paste("Y_ref ~", paste(c(exposure, cov_full), collapse = " + ")))
  f_red <- as.formula(paste("Y_ref ~", paste(c(exposure, cov_red), collapse = " + ")))
  m_full <- tryCatch(lm(f_full, data = d), error = function(e) NULL)
  m_red <- tryCatch(lm(f_red, data = d), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(m_full) || is.null(m_red)) return(c(NA_real_, nrow(d), NA_integer_))
  b_full <- coef(m_full)[exposure]
  b_red <- coef(m_red)[exposure]
  if (is.na(b_full) || is.na(b_red) || !is.finite(b_full) || abs(b_full) < 1e-8) return(c(NA_real_, nrow(d), nrow(model.frame(m_red))))
  c(abs((b_red - b_full) / b_full) * 100, nrow(model.frame(m_full)), nrow(model.frame(m_red)))
}

      n_full = as.integer(stats_vec[2]),
      n_drop_covariate = as.integer(stats_vec[3]),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
}))

      Covariate %in% sensitivity_covars_husna ~ "sensitivity",
      grepl("^CFastingHoursBloodSample", Covariate) ~ "sampling_condition",
      TRUE ~ "other"

  ) %>%

    retain_signal = ifelse(!is.na(beta_change_pct) & beta_change_pct > 10, "yes", "no"),
    suggested_status = case_when(
      role == "core" ~ "keep_core",
      any_p20 == "yes" | retain_signal == "yes" ~ "keep_sensitivity",
      TRUE ~ "consider_drop"
    )
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    Panel,
    Covariate,
    Role = role,
    `Any p < 0.20` = any_p20,
    `Ref beta change %` = round(beta_change_pct, 1),
    `N full` = n_full,
    `N drop covariate` = n_drop_covariate,
    `Collinearity flag` = collinearity_flag,
    `Suggested status` = suggested_status
  ) %>%
  arrange(Panel, Covariate)

if (nrow(drop_candidates) > 0) {
  cat("- **Candidates for Omission**: The following sensitivity covariates did not meet the screening signal thresholds (p < 0.20 or >10% reference beta change). They are flagged for possible omission to simplify the models, prevent over-specification, and maximize complete-case sample size:\n")
  for (pnl in unique(drop_candidates$Panel)) {
    vars_pnl <- drop_candidates$Covariate[drop_candidates$Panel == pnl]
    
    # Calculate potential sample size recovery
    pnl_df <- decision_tbl_husna %>% filter(Panel == pnl, Covariate %in% vars_pnl)
    n_recovered <- max(pnl_df$`N drop covariate` - pnl_df$`N full`, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    cat(sprintf("  - *%s*: %s (Omitting these recovers up to **%d** complete cases)\n", 
                pnl, paste(vars_pnl, collapse = ", "), if(is.finite(n_recovered) && n_recovered > 0) as.integer(n_recovered) else 0L))
  }
} else {
  cat("- **Candidates for Omission**: No sensitivity covariates met the automated omission criteria under the default signal thresholds. However, to prevent model over-specification (overfitting) and preserve degrees of freedom, you should still evaluate whether retaining all sensitivity adjusters is justified by the clinical context. If they do not materially alter the exposure estimates, sticking to the minimal core DAG set is recommended.\n")
}


Candidates for Omission: No sensitivity covariates met the automated omission criteria under the default signal thresholds. However, to prevent model over-specification (overfitting) and preserve degrees of freedom, you should still evaluate whether retaining all sensitivity adjusters is justified by the clinical context. If they do not materially alter the exposure estimates, sticking to the minimal core DAG set is recommended.

3.15 Missingness
Missingness is reported as a data-quality diagnostic and to inform interpretation of precision and sensitivity analyses within the visit-linked analytic panels.


Show code
covars <- unique(c(core_covars_husna, sensitivity_covars_husna, paste0("CFastingHoursBloodSample", visit_levels_husna)))

missing_by_panel <- bind_rows(lapply(names(clinical_visit_map_husna), function(vn) {
  df_panel <- clinical_visit_map_husna[[vn]]
  vars_here <- covars[covars %in% names(df_panel)]
  if (length(vars_here) == 0) return(NULL)

  data.frame(
    visit = gsub("visit", "visit ", vn),
    Covariate = vars_here,
    Available_N = sapply(vars_here, function(v) sum(!is.na(df_panel[[v]]))),
    Missing_pct = sapply(vars_here, function(v) round(mean(is.na(df_panel[[v]])) * 100, 1)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

missing_by_panel %>%
  arrange(visit, desc(Missing_pct), Covariate) %>%
  show_table(
    page_len = 10,
    title = "Covariate missingness by visit-linked panel",
    caption = "Covariate missingness by visit-linked panel"
  )


Covariate missingness by visit-linked panel

title: Covariate and model specification
text:
4 Model specification

4.1 Proposed models

Age-specific linear model: Y_age ~ E + maternal_core_covariates + visit_specific_fasting
Mutually adjusted timing model: Y_age ~ E_early + E_late + maternal_core_covariates + visit_specific_fasting
Repeated-measures mixed model: Y_it ~ E + age_t + E:age_t + maternal_core_covariates + (1|child)
Sensitivity models: add sensitivity covariate block and compare estimates.



4.2 Mathematical model forms

Age-specific linear model (age a):

\[
Y_{i,a} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 E_i + \sum_{k=1}^{p} \gamma_k C_{i,k} + \delta F_{i,a} + \varepsilon_{i,a}
\]

Mutually adjusted timing model (age a):

\[
Y_{i,a} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 E_{i,\mathrm{early}} + \beta_2 E_{i,\mathrm{late}} + \sum_{k=1}^{p} \gamma_k C_{i,k} + \delta F_{i,a} + \varepsilon_{i,a}
\]

Repeated-measures mixed model (child i, time t):

\[
Y_{i,t} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 E_i + \beta_2 t + \beta_3(E_i \times t) + \sum_{k=1}^{p} \gamma_k C_{i,k} + u_i + \varepsilon_{i,t}
\]
with random intercept:
\[
u_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma_u^2), \quad \varepsilon_{i,t} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2)
\]

4.4 Variable key


Click to expand full variable key



Show code
build_variable_key <- function(df) {
  nm <- names(df)
  make_friendly <- function(x) {
    y <- gsub("([a-z])([A-Z])", "\\1 \\2", x)
    y <- gsub("([A-Za-z])([0-9])", "\\1 \\2", y)
    y <- gsub("([0-9])([A-Za-z])", "\\1 \\2", y)
    y <- gsub("[._]+", " ", y)
    y <- gsub("\\s+", " ", trimws(y))
    tools::toTitleCase(tolower(y))
  }
  var_label <- sapply(nm, function(v) {
    lb <- attr(df[[v]], "label")
    if (is.null(lb) || is.na(lb) || lb == "") "" else as.character(lb)
  })
  value_levels <- sapply(nm, function(v) {
    labs <- attr(df[[v]], "labels")
    if (is.null(labs) || length(labs) == 0) return("")
    paste(sprintf("%s = %s", names(labs), unname(labs)), collapse = "; ")
  })
  data.frame(
    Friendly_Name = sapply(nm, make_friendly),
    Variable_Code = nm,
    Variable_Label = var_label,
    Value_Levels = value_levels,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

variable_key <- build_variable_key(clinical)
show_table(
  variable_key,
  page_len = 10,
  title = "Variable key",
  caption = "Variable key"
)

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
text: These figures show exposure-level detail, timing-window comparison, and robustness patterns for the age-specific and mutual-timing models. They are not the primary inferential summaries and should not be read as direct evidence for the warning-prone Q4 longitudinal model family unless stated explicitly.

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
text:
1.1 Figure B1. Global association screen


Show code
screen_df <- bind_rows(
  early_res %>% filter(exposure == rep_early) %>% mutate(window = "Early pregnancy"),
  late_res %>% filter(exposure == rep_late) %>% mutate(window = "Late pregnancy")
) %>%
  add_feature_meta() %>%
  group_by(window, visit) %>%
  arrange(p.value, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(rank = row_number(), logp = safe_logp(p.value)) %>%
  ungroup()

screen_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rank, y = logp, color = feature_group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  facet_grid(window ~ visit, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    x = "Ordered metabolite rank",
    y = "-log10(p)",
    color = "Metabolite class",
    title = "Global association screens by pregnancy window and child age panel"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16)








Figure 1: Global association screen across pregnancy windows (Early/Late) and child age panels (6mo to 5-6y). Points indicate -log10(p-values) for all metabolites, colored by the new 5 biological classes.

section: 1.3 Figure B3. Timing comparison (early vs late)
text:
1.3 Figure B3. Timing comparison (early vs late)


Show code
timing_df <- mutual_res %>%
  add_feature_meta() %>%
  mutate(best_p = pmin(p_early, p_late, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  group_by(visit) %>%
  slice_min(best_p, n = 12, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(visit, feature, feature_group, estimate_early, estimate_late) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(estimate_early, estimate_late),
    names_to = "window",
    values_to = "estimate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    window = recode(window, estimate_early = "Early pregnancy", estimate_late = "Late pregnancy")
  )

timing_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = window, y = estimate, group = feature, color = feature_group)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_point(size = 1.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ visit) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mutually adjusted beta",
    color = "Metabolite class",
    title = "Timing comparison of early vs late pregnancy estimates"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16)








Figure 3: Timing comparison (ladder plot) of mutually adjusted beta coefficients for early vs. late pregnancy diet measurements, colored by the 5 biological classes.

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 1.4 Figure B4. Age-pattern comparison across child visits
text:
1.4 Figure B4. Age-pattern comparison across child visits


Show code
base_early <- unique(strip_window_suffix(early_res$exposure))
base_late <- unique(strip_window_suffix(late_res$exposure))
common_base <- intersect(base_early, base_late)

base_rank <- bind_rows(
  early_res %>% mutate(base = strip_window_suffix(exposure)),
  late_res %>% mutate(base = strip_window_suffix(exposure))
) %>%
  filter(base %in% common_base) %>%
  group_by(base) %>%
  summarise(best_q = min(q.value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(best_q)

selected_base <- if (nrow(base_rank) > 0) base_rank$base[[1]] else NA_character_

exp_early <- early_res %>%
  filter(strip_window_suffix(exposure) == selected_base) %>%
  group_by(exposure) %>%
  summarise(best_q = min(q.value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(best_q) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  pull(exposure)

exp_late <- late_res %>%
  filter(strip_window_suffix(exposure) == selected_base) %>%
  group_by(exposure) %>%
  summarise(best_q = min(q.value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(best_q) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  pull(exposure)

traj_df <- bind_rows(
  early_res %>% filter(exposure == exp_early) %>% mutate(window = "Early pregnancy"),
  late_res %>% filter(exposure == exp_late) %>% mutate(window = "Late pregnancy")
) %>%
  add_feature_meta()

top_traj_feat <- traj_df %>%
  group_by(feature) %>%
  summarise(best_q = min(q.value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  slice_min(best_q, n = 8, with_ties = FALSE)

traj_plot <- traj_df %>%
  semi_join(top_traj_feat, by = "feature") %>%
  mutate(
    visit_age = factor(visit, levels = c(4, 5, 6, 7), labels = c("6 mo", "12 mo", "24 mo", "5-6 y")),
    feature_lab = paste0(feature_group, " | ", feature)
  )

traj_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = visit_age, y = estimate, color = window, group = window)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 1.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.1, linewidth = 0.35) +
  facet_wrap(~ feature_lab, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Child age panel",
    y = "Standardized beta (95% CI)",
    color = "Pregnancy window",
    title = paste0("Age-specific longitudinal comparison for exposure family: ", selected_base)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)








Figure 4: Age-specific comparison across child visits for one matched exposure family. This panel visualizes age-specific betas and confidence intervals, colored by class.

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 1.5 Figure B5. Sensitivity stability by window and visit
text:
1.5 Figure B5. Sensitivity stability by window and visit


Show code
stability_tbl <- bind_rows(
  compare_primary_sensitivity(early_res, early_res_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(window = "Early pregnancy"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(late_res, late_res_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(window = "Late pregnancy")
) %>%
  group_by(window, visit) %>%
  summarise(
    pairs_compared = n(),
    median_abs_delta = median(abs_delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_reclassification_fraction = mean(q_reclassified, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    median_abs_delta = round(median_abs_delta, 3),
    q_reclassification_fraction = round(q_reclassification_fraction, 3),
    visit_lab = factor(visit, levels = c(4, 5, 6, 7), labels = c("6 mo", "12 mo", "24 mo", "5-6 y"))
  )

make_table(stability_tbl, caption = "Age-specific sensitivity stability summary")










Figure 5: Stability of age-specific study estimates under expanded covariate adjustment. Reclassification uses the q < 0.10 threshold.




Show code
stability_tbl %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(median_abs_delta, q_reclassification_fraction), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
    median_abs_delta = "Median absolute beta change",
    q_reclassification_fraction = "Fraction reclassified at q < 0.10"
  )) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = visit_lab, y = value, fill = window)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Child age panel",
    y = "Value",
    fill = "Window",
    title = "Sensitivity stability across study age-specific models"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)








Figure 6: Stability of age-specific study estimates under expanded covariate adjustment. Reclassification uses the q < 0.10 threshold.

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 1.6 Figure B6. Metabolite-class composition of significant hits
text:
1.6 Figure B6. Metabolite-class composition of significant hits


Show code
class_comp <- bind_rows(
  early_res %>% filter(q.value < 0.1) %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q1 early | visit ", visit)),
  late_res %>% filter(q.value < 0.1) %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q2 late | visit ", visit)),
  mutual_res %>% filter(q_early < 0.1) %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q3 early term | visit ", visit)),
  mutual_res %>% filter(q_late < 0.1) %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q3 late term | visit ", visit))
) %>%
  add_feature_meta() %>%
  count(component, feature_group, name = "n_hits") %>%
  group_by(component) %>%
  mutate(hit_fraction = n_hits / sum(n_hits)) %>%
  ungroup()

make_table(
  class_comp %>% mutate(hit_fraction = round(hit_fraction, 3)),
  caption = "Metabolite-class composition of significant study hits"
)










Figure 7: Metabolite-class composition of significant associations (q < 0.10) across study age-specific and mutual-timing components, grouped by the 5 biological classes.




Show code
class_comp %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = component, y = hit_fraction, fill = feature_group)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of significant hits",
    fill = "Metabolite class",
    title = "Class composition of significant study findings"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)








Figure 8: Metabolite-class composition of significant associations (q < 0.10) across study age-specific and mutual-timing components, grouped by the 5 biological classes.

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
text:
1.7 Figure B7. Metabolite correlation heatmap


Show code
corr_df <- build_corr_heatmap_df(df_all, metabolite_cols)

if (is.null(corr_df)) {
  ggplot() +
    annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "Metabolite correlation heatmap unavailable") +
    theme_void()
} else {
  ggplot(corr_df, aes(x = col, y = row, fill = correlation)) +
    geom_raster() +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "#2166AC",
      mid = "white",
      high = "#B2182B",
      midpoint = 0,
      limits = c(-1, 1)
    ) +
    coord_equal() +
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = NULL,
      fill = "r",
      title = "Metabolite correlation structure"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks = element_blank(),
      panel.grid = element_blank()
    )
}








Figure 9: Correlation heatmap across all 250 metabolite features. Correlations are computed from the pooled standardized metabolite matrix and ordered by hierarchical clustering.

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 2.1 Figure B8. Global association heatmap of biological metabolite groups
text:
2.1 Figure B8. Global association heatmap of biological metabolite groups
This heatmap displays the standardized beta coefficients of all early and late pregnancy dietary variables with the 5 biological group scores across the child visits.


Show code
heatmap_df <- early_late_sec %>%
  filter(note == "ok") %>%
  mutate(
    group_lbl = label_group(feature),
    exposure_lbl = label_exposure_detailed(exposure),
    visit_lbl = factor(visit, levels = c(4, 5, 6, 7), labels = c("6 mo", "12 mo", "24 mo", "5-6 y")),
    label_sig = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, sprintf("%.3f", estimate), "")
  )

ggplot(heatmap_df, aes(x = group_lbl, y = exposure_lbl, fill = estimate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label_sig), size = 3, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#313695", mid = "#ffffbf", high = "#a50026",
    midpoint = 0, name = "Beta (SD)"
  ) +
  facet_grid(window ~ visit_lbl) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.title = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(title = "Standardized beta coefficients for biological metabolite groups")








Figure 10: Heatmap of standardized beta coefficients between maternal diet and biological metabolite groups. Cells show beta values when p < 0.05.

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 2.2 Figure B9. Group-level forest plots for representative exposures
text:
2.2 Figure B9. Group-level forest plots for representative exposures
This forest plot displays the standardized beta coefficients (and 95% confidence intervals) for the associations of the representative early pregnancy (% Energy omega-6 1) and late pregnancy (DII (Normal Diet) 2) dietary exposures on the 5 biological metabolite groups across visits.


Show code
forest_df_sec <- early_late_sec %>%
  filter(
    note == "ok",
    (window == "Early pregnancy" & exposure == rep_early) |
    (window == "Late pregnancy" & exposure == rep_late)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    group_lbl = label_group(feature),
    visit_lbl = factor(visit, levels = c(4, 5, 6, 7), labels = c("6 mo", "12 mo", "24 mo", "5-6 y"))
  )

ggplot(forest_df_sec, aes(x = estimate, y = group_lbl, color = group_lbl)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3.5) +
  facet_grid(window ~ visit_lbl) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "grey85")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Standardized beta coefficient (95% CI)",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Associations of maternal dietary patterns with child metabolite group scores"
  )








Figure 11: Forest plots showing group score standardized betas (95% CI) for maternal dietary patterns.

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 2.3 Figure B10. Mutual timing comparison on biological group scores
text:
2.3 Figure B10. Mutual timing comparison on biological group scores
Ladder plot comparing mutually adjusted beta coefficients for early vs. late pregnancy diet measurements (Q6) on the 5 biological groups.


Show code
timing_df_sec <- mutual_sec %>%
  filter(note == "ok", exposure_pair == strip_window_suffix(rep_early)) %>%
  mutate(
    group_lbl = label_group(feature),
    visit_lbl = factor(visit, levels = c(4, 5, 6, 7), labels = c("6 mo", "12 mo", "24 mo", "5-6 y"))
  ) %>%
  select(visit_lbl, group_lbl, estimate_early, estimate_late) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(estimate_early, estimate_late),
    names_to = "window",
    values_to = "estimate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    window = recode(window, estimate_early = "Early pregnancy", estimate_late = "Late pregnancy")
  )

ggplot(timing_df_sec, aes(x = window, y = estimate, group = group_lbl, color = group_lbl)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~ visit_lbl, ncol = 4) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mutually adjusted beta",
    color = "Metabolite group",
    title = paste("Early vs. Late Pregnancy mutual timing comparison for", label_exposure_detailed(strip_window_suffix(rep_early)))
  )

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 2.4 Figure B11. Sex-stratified effects for significant Diet × Sex interactions
text:
2.4 Figure B11. Sex-stratified effects for significant Diet × Sex interactions
Sex-stratified analyses are conducted and presented separately in boys (blue) and girls (red) ONLY for outcome-exposure pairs that demonstrated a significant Diet × Sex interaction (\(p_{\text{interaction}} < 0.05\)).


Show code
# Get significant interaction pairs
sig_interactions <- sex_int_sec %>%
  filter(note == "ok", !is.na(interaction_p), interaction_p < 0.05)

if (nrow(sig_interactions) == 0 || nrow(sex_strat_sec) == 0) {
  ggplot() +
    annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "No significant Diet x Sex interactions (p < 0.05) found in group-level models.") +
    theme_void()
} else {
  # sex_strat_sec has columns: outcome, exposure, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value, sex_level, visit
  strat_plot_df <- sex_strat_sec %>%
    filter(note == "ok") %>%
    mutate(
      feature = gsub("^grp_", "", outcome),
      group_lbl = label_group(feature),
      exposure_lbl = label_exposure_detailed(exposure),
      label = paste0("Visit ", visit, " | ", group_lbl, "\nvs. ", exposure_lbl),
      sex = ifelse(sex_level == "1", "Boys", "Girls")
    )
  
  ggplot(strat_plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = label, color = sex)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2, linewidth = 0.9, position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
    geom_point(size = 3.5, position = position_dodge(0.4)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Boys" = "#2c7bb6", "Girls" = "#d7191c")) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      axis.title.y = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Sex-stratified standardized beta (95% CI)",
      color = "Child sex",
      title = "Maternal diet associations stratified by child sex (biological groups)"
    )
}








Figure 13: Sex-stratified standardized beta coefficients (95% CI) in boys and girls for groups showing significant Diet x Sex interaction (p < 0.05).

title: Secondary figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 2.5 Figure B12. Correlation heatmap of biological group scores
text:
2.5 Figure B12. Correlation heatmap of biological group scores
Correlation heatmap visualising the relationships and redundancies between the 5 biological group scores at Visit 4 (6 mo).


Show code
# Compute group scores for visit 4 from MAE
source("../../scripts/metabolomics_common.R")
tse_v4 <- experiments(mae)[["visit_4"]]
mat_v4 <- assay(tse_v4, "mbo")
feat_map <- results_sec$feature_map
feat_avail <- intersect(feat_map$feature[!is.na(feat_map$group)], rownames(mat_v4))

# Z-score and average within groups
grp_names <- unique(na.omit(feat_map$group))
scores_v4 <- data.frame(row.names = colnames(mat_v4))
for (grp in grp_names) {
  members <- feat_map$feature[feat_map$group == grp & !is.na(feat_map$group)]
  members <- intersect(members, rownames(mat_v4))
  if (length(members) == 0) next
  sub <- mat_v4[members, , drop = FALSE]
  sub_z <- t(apply(sub, 1, function(x) {
    x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
    sx <- sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (!is.finite(sx) || sx == 0) return(rep(NA_real_, length(x)))
    (x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / sx
  }))
  scores_v4[[grp]] <- colMeans(sub_z, na.rm = TRUE)
}

names(scores_v4) <- label_group(names(scores_v4))
cor_v4 <- cor(scores_v4, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

cor_long_sec <- as.data.frame(cor_v4) %>%
  mutate(group1 = rownames(.)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -group1, names_to = "group2", values_to = "correlation")

ggplot(cor_long_sec, aes(x = group1, y = group2, fill = correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", correlation)), size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#2166ac", mid = "#f7f7f7", high = "#b2182b",
    midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), name = "Correlation (r)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(title = "Metabolite group correlation structure (Visit 4)")








Figure 14: Correlation heatmap of the 5 biological group scores at Visit 4.

title: Primary results: maternal diet and child metabolites
text: Maternal prenatal diet is related to child metabolomics from 6 months to 5-6 years. The age-specific and mutual-timing models carry the main inferential weight; the longitudinal component now returns coefficient-level output across all attempted exposures but remains more warning-prone and assumption-sensitive.

title: Primary results: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 5 Q4 Longitudinal diet-by-age interaction models across childhood
text:
5 Q4 Longitudinal diet-by-age interaction models across childhood
Evaluation of whether prenatal diet is associated with metabolite trajectories across childhood (Diet \(\times\) Age interaction). Complete-case n across fitted models: 482-727.
This is still the most warning-prone component. The saved longitudinal result object contains 8996 coefficient rows from 52 attempted longitudinal exposure runs, and the executed models generated substantial warnings. This primary-results section intentionally displays the Diet x Age interaction coefficients, because those are the direct Q4 longitudinal estimand in this report. The full longitudinal coefficient set, including the main exposure terms from the same mixed-effects models, is available in the exposure atlas.

6 Sensitivity summary


Show code
age_comp <- bind_rows(
  compare_primary_sensitivity(early_res, early_res_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(window = "Early"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(late_res, late_res_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(window = "Late"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(mutual_early_res, mutual_early_res_sens, c("exposure_pair", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(window = "Q3 mutual timing: early term"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(mutual_late_res, mutual_late_res_sens, c("exposure_pair", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(window = "Q3 mutual timing: late term")
)

if (nrow(age_comp) > 0) {
  age_summary <- age_comp %>%
    group_by(window, exposure, visit) %>%
    summarise(
      pairs_compared = n(),
      median_abs_delta = median(abs_delta, na.rm = TRUE),
      p90_abs_delta = as.numeric(quantile(abs_delta, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
      direction_flip_fraction = mean(direction_flip, na.rm = TRUE),
      q_reclassification_fraction = mean(q_reclassified, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      median_abs_delta = round(median_abs_delta, 3),
      p90_abs_delta = round(p90_abs_delta, 3),
      direction_flip_fraction = round(direction_flip_fraction, 3),
      q_reclassification_fraction = round(q_reclassification_fraction, 3)
    )

  datatable(
    age_summary,
    caption = "Primary-vs-sensitivity estimate stability",
    filter = "top",
    options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX = FALSE, autoWidth = FALSE)
  )
} else {
  message("Sensitivity comparison unavailable until sensitivity outputs are generated.")
}

title: Study overview
text:
6 Multiple testing
False-discovery control is based on Benjamini-Hochberg adjustment across metabolites, but the adjustment scope differs by model family.

Age-specific outputs apply BH within each visit-specific exposure model.
Mutual timing outputs apply BH separately to the early-term and late-term coefficients within each visit-specific exposure pair.
Longitudinal outputs apply BH separately within each exposure and coefficient family.

title: Study overview
text:
7 Result inventory

late_all <- flatten_age_specific(results$age_specific, "late")

mutual_late_all <- flatten_mutual(results$age_specific, "late")

  ),

8 Execution status

  mutate(
    question = dplyr::case_when(
      question == "Q4" ~ "Q1: Early-Pregnancy Age-Specific Models",

title: Study overview
text:
9 Interpretation notes

The age-specific and mutual-timing models carry the clearest inferential weight in the current Study 2 package.
Prenatal exposures are baseline pregnancy-window variables; child-age rows represent linked analytic panels, not repeated prenatal measurements.
The longitudinal interaction model is part of the target question set and now returns coefficient-level output across all attempted exposures, but it should still be interpreted more cautiously than the age-specific models because the fits generated substantial warnings.
Detailed coefficient tables, sensitivity comparisons, and supporting figures are reported elsewhere in the site; this page is the study-level map.

  analytic_step = c(
    "Children with at least one linked metabolomics panel",
    "Children with a 6-month metabolomics panel",
    "Children with a 12-month metabolomics panel",
    "Children with a 24-month metabolomics panel",
    "Children with a 5-6-year metabolomics panel",
    "Children with any linked early-pregnancy exposure",
    "Children with any linked late-pregnancy exposure",
    "Children with both early- and late-pregnancy exposures in linked panels",
    "Children contributing at least two child-age panels"
  ),
  n_children = c(
    n_distinct(raw_df$StudyID),
    n_distinct(raw_df$StudyID[raw_df$visit_num == 4]),
    n_distinct(raw_df$StudyID[raw_df$visit_num == 5]),
    n_distinct(raw_df$StudyID[raw_df$visit_num == 6]),
    n_distinct(raw_df$StudyID[raw_df$visit_num == 7]),
    n_distinct(raw_df$StudyID[raw_df$has_early_exposure]),
    n_distinct(raw_df$StudyID[raw_df$has_late_exposure]),
    n_distinct(raw_df$StudyID[raw_df$has_early_exposure & raw_df$has_late_exposure]),
    sum(child_panel_counts$n_panels >= 2, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)

make_table(sample_tbl, caption = "Supplementary Table S1. Analytic sample derivation")

title: Supplementary tables
text:
2 Supplementary Table S2. Exposure definitions


Show code
exposure_tbl <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    component = "Q1: Early-Pregnancy Age-Specific Models",
    exposure_timing = "Early pregnancy",
    exposure_code = q4_expos
  ),
  tibble(
    component = "Q2: Late-Pregnancy Age-Specific Models",
    exposure_timing = "Late pregnancy",
    exposure_code = q5_expos
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    exposure_label = pretty_var(exposure_code),
    scale = case_when(
      grepl("DietaryPatterns", exposure_code) ~ "Diet pattern score",
      grepl("DII", exposure_code) ~ "Diet inflammatory index score",
      grepl("IDQ", exposure_code) ~ "Diet quality score",
      TRUE ~ "Continuous percentage or intake metric"
    ),
    linked_use = case_when(
      grepl("1$", exposure_code) ~ "Used in early-pregnancy age-specific, mutual-timing, and early longitudinal attempts",
      grepl("2$", exposure_code) ~ "Used in late-pregnancy age-specific, mutual-timing, and late longitudinal attempts",
      TRUE ~ "Used in prenatal diet modeling"
    )
  ) %>%
  relocate(exposure_label, .after = exposure_code)

make_table(exposure_tbl, caption = "Supplementary Table S2. Exposure definitions and coding")

text:
5 Supplementary Table S5. Candidate associations with q < 0.10


Show code
candidate_age_specific <- bind_rows(
  early_all %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q1 early pregnancy | ", visit_label(visit))),
  late_all %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q2 late pregnancy | ", visit_label(visit)))
) %>%
  filter(q.value < 0.10) %>%
  transmute(
    component,
    exposure = pretty_var(exposure),
    feature,
    n,
    beta = estimate,
    conf_low = conf.low,
    conf_high = conf.high,
    p_value = p.value,
    q_value = q.value
  )

candidate_mutual <- bind_rows(
  mutual_early_all %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q3 mutual timing early term | ", visit_label(visit))),
  mutual_late_all %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q3 mutual timing late term | ", visit_label(visit)))
) %>%
  filter(q.value < 0.10) %>%
  transmute(
    component,
    exposure = pretty_var(exposure_pair),
    feature,
    n,
    beta = estimate,
    conf_low = conf.low,
    conf_high = conf.high,
    p_value = p.value,
    q_value = q.value
  )

candidate_tbl <- bind_rows(candidate_age_specific, candidate_mutual) %>%
  arrange(q_value, p_value)
