title: Supplementary tables
section: 6 Supplementary Table S6. Primary-vs-sensitivity stability summary
text:
6 Supplementary Table S6. Primary-vs-sensitivity stability summary


Show code
long_coef_primary <- long_res %>% filter("feature" %in% names(long_res))
long_coef_sens <- long_res_sens %>% filter("feature" %in% names(long_res_sens))

stability_tbl <- bind_rows(
  compare_primary_sensitivity(early_all, early_all_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Q1: Early-Pregnancy Age-Specific Models"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(late_all, late_all_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Q2: Late-Pregnancy Age-Specific Models"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(mutual_early_all, mutual_early_all_sens, c("exposure_pair", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Q3: Mutual Timing: Early-Pregnancy Term"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(mutual_late_all, mutual_late_all_sens, c("exposure_pair", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Q3: Mutual Timing: Late-Pregnancy Term"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(long_coef_primary, long_coef_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "term")) %>% mutate(component = "Q4: Longitudinal Interaction Model")
) %>%
  group_by(component) %>%
  summarise(
    matched_rows = n(),
    median_abs_beta_change = median(abs_delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90_abs_beta_change = quantile(abs_delta, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
    direction_flip_fraction = mean(direction_flip, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_reclassification_fraction = mean(q_reclassified, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

make_table(stability_tbl, caption = "Supplementary Table S6. Primary-vs-sensitivity stability summary")

title: Supplementary tables
section: 7 Supplementary Table S7. Longitudinal execution status inventory
text:
7 Supplementary Table S7. Longitudinal execution status inventory


Show code
status_tbl <- model_status %>%
  mutate(
    component = case_when(
      question == "Q4" ~ "Q1: Early-Pregnancy Age-Specific Models",
      question == "Q5" ~ "Q2: Late-Pregnancy Age-Specific Models",
      question == "Q6" ~ "Q3: Mutual Timing Model",
      question == "Q7" ~ "Q4: Longitudinal Interaction Model",
      TRUE ~ question
    ),
    child_age = ifelse(is.na(visit), NA_character_, visit_label(as.integer(visit))),
    exposure = pretty_var(exposure),
    complete_case_n = ifelse(
      is.na(n_complete_min) | !is.finite(n_complete_min),
      NA_character_,
      ifelse(n_complete_min == n_complete_max, as.character(n_complete_min), paste0(n_complete_min, "-", n_complete_max))
    )
  ) %>%
  select(component, analysis, child_age, timing, exposure, term_family, status, reason, complete_case_n, n_features_tested)

make_table(status_tbl, caption = "Supplementary Table S7. Saved execution-status inventory")

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
text: Figures from the early-pregnancy, late-pregnancy, mutual-timing, and longitudinal analyses. The longitudinal component now returns coefficient-level output across all attempted exposures, but it still generated substantial execution warnings, so the figures below should be read as supporting context rather than a strong standalone inferential block.

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
text:
1 Analytic sample structure


Show code
visit_counts <- data.frame(
  visit = c(4, 5, 6, 7),
  n = c(
    nrow(as.data.frame(colData(experiments(mae)[["visit_4"]]))),
    nrow(as.data.frame(colData(experiments(mae)[["visit_5"]]))),
    nrow(as.data.frame(colData(experiments(mae)[["visit_6"]]))),
    nrow(as.data.frame(colData(experiments(mae)[["visit_7"]])))
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
visit_counts$child_age <- visit_label(visit_counts$visit)

visit_counts %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = child_age, y = n, fill = child_age)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.25, size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    x = "Child age",
    y = "Participants"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)








Figure 1: Visit-level sample sizes for the metabolomics analytical population. N indicates the number of unique participants with valid serum metabolite profiles at each study visit.







Show code
all_ids <- bind_rows(lapply(c(4, 5, 6, 7), function(v) {
  cd <- as.data.frame(colData(experiments(mae)[[paste0("visit_", v)]]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  data.frame(StudyID = as.character(cd$StudyID), visit = v, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}))

profile_tbl <- all_ids %>%
  distinct(StudyID, visit) %>%
  count(StudyID, name = "n_visits") %>%
  count(n_visits, name = "n_children")

profile_tbl %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(n_visits), y = n_children)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#1B6CA8", width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_children), vjust = -0.25, size = 3.3) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of visits contributed",
    y = "Children"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)








Figure 2: Participation profile across visits. This plot shows the number of children who contributed to one, two, three, or all four metabolomics panels.







Show code
cc_tbl <- bind_rows(
  early_res %>% distinct(timing, exposure, visit, n),
  late_res %>% distinct(timing, exposure, visit, n)
)

cc_tbl %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(visit), y = n, color = timing)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.35, width = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.55) +
  labs(
    x = "Child age",
    y = "Complete-case n",
    color = "Window"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = visit_label)








Figure 3: Complete-case sample sizes for regression models. Each point represents one exposure-metabolite pair, showing the final N used after aligning diet data and confounders.

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 2 Maternal exposure distributions and timing windows
text:
2 Maternal exposure distributions and timing windows


Show code
cd4 <- as.data.frame(colData(experiments(mae)[["visit_4"]]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

exp_early <- sort(unique(early_res$exposure))
exp_late <- sort(unique(late_res$exposure))

num_early <- exp_early[vapply(exp_early, function(v) v %in% names(cd4) && is.numeric(cd4[[v]]), logical(1))]
num_late <- exp_late[vapply(exp_late, function(v) v %in% names(cd4) && is.numeric(cd4[[v]]), logical(1))]

dist_early <- bind_rows(lapply(num_early, function(v) {
  data.frame(window = "Early", exposure = v, value = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(cd4[[v]])), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}))

dist_late <- bind_rows(lapply(num_late, function(v) {
  data.frame(window = "Late", exposure = v, value = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(cd4[[v]])), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}))

dist_all <- bind_rows(dist_early, dist_late)

if (nrow(dist_all) > 0) {
  dist_all %>%
    filter(is.finite(value)) %>%
    mutate(exposure = label_exposure(exposure)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = exposure, y = value, fill = window)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
    coord_flip() +
    facet_wrap(~ window, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = "Value"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
}








Figure 4: Distributions of maternal dietary exposures in early and late pregnancy. Standardized E% values are shown for all nutrients included in the targeted models.







Show code
pair_key <- intersect(strip_window_suffix(exp_early), strip_window_suffix(exp_late))

pair_df <- bind_rows(lapply(pair_key, function(k) {
  e <- exp_early[strip_window_suffix(exp_early) == k][1]
  l <- exp_late[strip_window_suffix(exp_late) == k][1]
  if (is.na(e) || is.na(l) || !(e %in% names(cd4)) || !(l %in% names(cd4))) return(NULL)

  data.frame(
    pair = k,
    early = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(cd4[[e]])),
    late = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(cd4[[l]])),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})) %>%
  filter(is.finite(early), is.finite(late))

if (nrow(pair_df) > 0) {
  pair_df %>%
    mutate(pair = label_exposure(pair)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = early, y = late)) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.55, size = 1.3, color = "#1B6CA8") +
    facet_wrap(~ pair, scales = "free") +
    labs(
      x = "Early pregnancy value",
      y = "Late pregnancy value"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
}








Figure 5: Intra-individual correlations between early and late pregnancy diet measurements. These pairings inform the mutual-timing models.

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
text:
3 Reported result rows


Show code
burden_tbl <- data.frame(
  component = c(
    "Q1 early pregnancy age-specific",
    "Q2 late pregnancy age-specific",
    "Q3 mutual timing",
    "Q4 longitudinal"
  ),
  result_rows = c(nrow(early_res), nrow(late_res), nrow(mutual_res), nrow(long_res)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

burden_tbl %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(component, result_rows), y = result_rows, fill = component)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(result_rows)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3.2) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Reported result rows") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16)








Figure 6: Count of reported result rows by analysis component. This avoids mixing feature-level association rows with exposure-level longitudinal attempts in one metric.

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
text:
4 Inferential signal summary


Show code
signal_tbl <- bind_rows(
  early_res %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q1 early pregnancy | ", visit_label(visit))) %>% group_by(component) %>% summarise(significant_rows = sum(q.value < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE), total_rows = n(), .groups = "drop"),
  late_res %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q2 late pregnancy | ", visit_label(visit))) %>% group_by(component) %>% summarise(significant_rows = sum(q.value < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE), total_rows = n(), .groups = "drop"),
  mutual_res %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q3 mutual timing: early term | ", visit_label(visit))) %>% group_by(component) %>% summarise(significant_rows = sum(q_early < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE), total_rows = n(), .groups = "drop"),
  mutual_res %>% mutate(component = paste0("Q3 mutual timing: late term | ", visit_label(visit))) %>% group_by(component) %>% summarise(significant_rows = sum(q_late < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE), total_rows = n(), .groups = "drop"),
  if (nrow(long_coef_res) > 0) {
    long_coef_res %>%
      group_by(timing) %>%
      summarise(component = paste0("Q4 longitudinal | ", timing), significant_rows = sum(q.value < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE), total_rows = n(), .groups = "drop")
  } else {
    data.frame(component = "Q4 longitudinal | no coefficient rows", significant_rows = 0L, total_rows = 0L, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
) %>%
  mutate(significant_fraction = ifelse(total_rows > 0, significant_rows / total_rows, NA_real_))

signal_tbl %>%
  filter(!is.na(significant_fraction)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(component, significant_fraction), y = significant_fraction, fill = component)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(scales::percent(significant_fraction, accuracy = 0.1), "\n(", significant_rows, "/", total_rows, ")")),
    hjust = -0.05,
    size = 2.9
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Fraction of rows with q < 0.10") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)








Figure 7: Significant result-row counts and fractions across analysis components. The mutual model is split into early-term and late-term summaries because it yields two coefficient families.

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
text:
5 Primary-vs-sensitivity stability


Show code
stability_comp <- bind_rows(
  compare_primary_sensitivity(early_res, early_res_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Q1 early pregnancy age-specific"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(late_res, late_res_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Q2 late pregnancy age-specific"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(mutual_early_res, mutual_early_res_sens, c("exposure_pair", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Q3 mutual timing: early term"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(mutual_late_res, mutual_late_res_sens, c("exposure_pair", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Q3 mutual timing: late term"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(long_coef_res, long_coef_res_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "term")) %>% mutate(component = "Q4 longitudinal")
)

if (nrow(stability_comp) > 0) {
  stability_tbl <- stability_comp %>%
    group_by(component) %>%
    summarise(
      pairs_compared = n(),
      median_abs_delta = median(abs_delta, na.rm = TRUE),
      q_reclassification_fraction = mean(q_reclassified, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      median_abs_delta = round(median_abs_delta, 3),
      q_reclassification_fraction = round(q_reclassification_fraction, 3)
    )

  stability_tbl %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(median_abs_delta, q_reclassification_fraction), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
    mutate(metric = recode(metric,
      median_abs_delta = "Median absolute beta change",
      q_reclassification_fraction = "Fraction reclassified at q < 0.10"
    )) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = component, y = value, fill = component)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(x = "Component", y = "Value") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
}








Figure 8: Visual summary of how much reported estimates move under expanded covariate adjustment. Reclassification uses the q < 0.10 threshold.

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 6 Volcano plots (representative early and late exposures)
text:
6 Volcano plots (representative early and late exposures)


Show code
rep_early <- pick_representative_exposure(early_res)
rep_late <- pick_representative_exposure(late_res)

volcano_df <- bind_rows(
  early_res %>% filter(exposure == rep_early) %>% mutate(window = "Early", sig = q.value < 0.1),
  late_res %>% filter(exposure == rep_late) %>% mutate(window = "Late", sig = q.value < 0.1)
) %>%
  mutate(logp = safe_logp(p.value))

volcano_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = logp, color = sig)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  facet_grid(window ~ visit, scales = "free") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey65", "TRUE" = "firebrick")) +
  labs(
    x = "Beta",
    y = "-log10(p)",
    color = "q < 0.10"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = visit_label)








Figure 9: Illustrative volcano plots for one post hoc exemplar exposure from the early and late pregnancy model families. Exemplar exposures are selected by the smallest observed q-value within each family and are not pre-specified headline exposures.

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
text:
7 Age-specific heatmaps


Show code
all_age_meta <- all_age %>%
  filter(is.finite(q.value)) %>%
  add_feature_meta()

top_exposures <- all_age_meta %>%
  group_by(timing, exposure) %>%
  summarise(best_q = min(q.value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(timing) %>%
  slice_min(best_q, n = 4, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

heat_pool <- all_age_meta %>%
  inner_join(top_exposures, by = c("timing", "exposure"))

top_features <- heat_pool %>%
  group_by(timing, feature, feature_group) %>%
  summarise(best_q = min(q.value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(timing) %>%
  slice_min(best_q, n = 20, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

heat_df <- heat_pool %>%
  inner_join(top_features, by = c("timing", "feature", "feature_group")) %>%
  mutate(
    panel = paste0(timing, " | ", label_exposure(exposure)),
    feature_lab = paste0(feature_group, " | ", feature)
  )

panel_order <- heat_df %>%
  group_by(panel) %>%
  summarise(best_q = min(q.value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(best_q) %>%
  pull(panel)

for (panel_i in panel_order) {
  panel_df <- heat_df %>%
    filter(panel == panel_i) %>%
    mutate(feature_lab = fct_reorder(feature_lab, q.value, .desc = FALSE))

  p <- panel_df %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = factor(visit), y = feature_lab, fill = estimate)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166AC", mid = "white", high = "#B2182B", midpoint = 0) +
    labs(
      x = "Child age",
      y = "Metabolite (Group | Feature)",
      fill = "Beta"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13)) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = visit_label)

  print(p)
}

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 8 Timing comparison: early vs late window
text:
8 Timing comparison: early vs late window


Show code
mutual_plot <- mutual_res %>%
  add_feature_meta() %>%
  mutate(best_p = pmin(p_early, p_late, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  group_by(visit) %>%
  slice_min(best_p, n = 60, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

mutual_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate_early, y = estimate_late, color = feature_group)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.6) +
  facet_wrap(~ visit) +
  labs(
    x = "Early-window beta",
    y = "Late-window beta",
    color = "Metabolite group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = visit_label)








Figure 18: Direct comparison of early vs. late pregnancy diet effect sizes for top-ranked associations. Points on the diagonal indicate consistent effects across windows.

title: Figures: maternal diet and child metabolites
section: 9 Q4 Longitudinal model status and descriptive support
text:
9 Q4 Longitudinal model status and descriptive support
The saved longitudinal output now covers all attempted longitudinal models, but these panels are still intended as cautionary context because the mixed-effects fits generated substantial warnings.


Show code
q7_attempt_tbl <- model_status %>%
  filter(question == "Q7") %>%
  count(timing, name = "attempted_exposures")

q7_return_tbl <- if (nrow(long_coef_res) > 0) {
  long_coef_res %>%
    count(timing, name = "returned_rows")
} else {
  q7_attempt_tbl %>% transmute(timing, returned_rows = 0L)
}

q7_yield_tbl <- full_join(q7_attempt_tbl, q7_return_tbl, by = "timing") %>%
  mutate(
    attempted_exposures = ifelse(is.na(attempted_exposures), 0L, attempted_exposures),
    returned_rows = ifelse(is.na(returned_rows), 0L, returned_rows)
  )

q7_yield_tbl %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(attempted_exposures, returned_rows), names_to = "quantity", values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(quantity = recode(quantity,
    attempted_exposures = "Attempted exposures",
    returned_rows = "Returned coefficient rows"
  )) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = timing, y = count, fill = quantity)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(aes(label = count), position = position_dodge(width = 0.65), vjust = -0.2, size = 3.1) +
  labs(x = "Timing window", y = "Count", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)








Figure 19: Q4 longitudinal output yield by timing window. Attempted exposures come from the saved execution log; returned coefficient rows come from the saved longitudinal result object.







Show code
status_tbl <- model_status %>%
  filter(question == "Q7") %>%
  count(timing, status, name = "n")

status_tbl %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = timing, y = n, fill = status)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.25, size = 3.3) +
  labs(
    x = "Timing window",
    y = "Exposure attempts",
    fill = "Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16)








Figure 20: Status report for longitudinal mixed-effects models based on the saved execution log. Status reflects whether saved output rows were produced, not whether the fits were warning-free.







Show code
# Descriptive trajectories are shown as a companion view to the age-specific results, not as a replacement for longitudinal-model inference.
best_row <- all_age %>%
  filter(is.finite(q.value)) %>%
  arrange(q.value) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1)

if (nrow(best_row) > 0) {
  exp0 <- best_row$exposure[[1]]
  feat0 <- best_row$feature[[1]]

  traj_df <- df_long %>%
    filter(visit_num %in% c(4, 5, 6, 7)) %>%
    mutate(
      exposure_value = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[exp0]])),
      metabolite_value = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[feat0]])),
      exposure_tertile = make_exposure_tertiles(exposure_value)
    ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(metabolite_value), !is.na(exposure_tertile)) %>%
    group_by(visit_num, exposure_tertile) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_value = mean(metabolite_value, na.rm = TRUE),
      se = sd(metabolite_value, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  traj_df %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = factor(visit_num), y = mean_value, color = exposure_tertile, group = exposure_tertile)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_value - 1.96 * se, ymax = mean_value + 1.96 * se), width = 0.1) +
    labs(
      x = "Child age",
      y = "Mean standardized metabolite",
      color = "Exposure tertile"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = visit_label)
}








Figure 21: Descriptive trajectories for the top age-specific association, shown by exposure tertile. This is a companion descriptive figure and is not itself a longitudinal-model result.

title: Clinical characteristics
text:
Show code
source("analysis_data_utils.R")
source("../../scripts/methods_common.R")
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

visits <- c(4L, 5L, 6L, 7L)

visit_label <- function(v) dplyr::case_when(
  v == 4L ~ "6 Months",
  v == 5L ~ "12 Months",
  v == 6L ~ "24 Months",
  v == 7L ~ "5-6 Years",
  TRUE ~ paste("Visit", v)
)

pretty_var <- function(x) {
  x <- sub("^M", "", x)
  x <- gsub("DietaryPatterns", "Dietary Patterns ", x)
  x <- gsub("DIINormalDiet", "Dietary Inflammatory Index ", x)
  x <- gsub("DIIDensityDiet", "Dietary Inflammatory Index Density ", x)
  x <- gsub("^IDQ1$", "IDQ", x)
  x <- gsub("^IDQ2$", "IDQ", x)
  x <- gsub("EPercent", "Percent ", x)
  x <- gsub("Fibtot", "Fibre ", x)
  x <- gsub("CHO", "Carbohydrate", x)
  x <- gsub("Pro", "Protein", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn3", "n-3 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn6", "n-6 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("SFA", "SFA", x)
  x <- gsub("MUFA", "MUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("PUFA", "PUFA", x)
  x <- sub("[12]$", "", x)
  x <- gsub("_", " ", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("\\s+", " ", trimws(x))
  tools::toTitleCase(tolower(x))
}

exposures_early <- c(
  "MDietaryPatterns_1", "MDIINormalDiet1", "MDIIDensityDiet1", "MIDQ1",
  "MEPercentProDiet1", "MEPercentCHODiet1", "MEPercentFatDiet1",
  "MEPercentPUFADiet1", "MEPercentMUFADiet1", "MEPercentSFADiet1",
  "MEPercentFAn3Diet1", "MEPercentFAn6Diet1", "MEPercentFibtotDiet1"
)
exposures_late <- c(
  "MDietaryPatterns_2", "MDIINormalDiet2", "MDIIDensityDiet2", "MIDQ2",
  "MEPercentProDiet2", "MEPercentCHODiet2", "MEPercentFatDiet2",
  "MEPercentPUFADiet2", "MEPercentMUFADiet2", "MEPercentSFADiet2",
  "MEPercentFAn3Diet2", "MEPercentFAn6Diet2", "MEPercentFibtotDiet2"
)
exposure_map <- list(
  "4" = c(exposures_early, exposures_late),
  "5" = c(exposures_early, exposures_late),
  "6" = c(exposures_early, exposures_late),
  "7" = c(exposures_early, exposures_late)
)

core_covars <- c(
  "Intervention", "CGender", "MEthnicity", "MDise"
)
sensitivity_covars <- c(
  "MprepBMI", "MAge1", "MprevGDM_new", "Bfdurationm",
  "MPRSmoke", "MUniEdu", "CWeightBirth"
)

analysis_obj <- prepare_analysis_data(
  mae_path = "../data/MAE_original.rds",
  visits = paste0("visit_", visits)
)

mae <- analysis_obj$mae

first_non_missing <- function(x) {
  idx <- which(!is.na(x))
  if (length(idx) == 0) return(NA)
  x[[idx[1]]]
}

title: Clinical characteristics
text:
2 Objective-specific context
Maternal baseline characteristics, prenatal diet exposures, and child-age analytic panel characteristics are summarized here.
Early- and late-pregnancy diet exposures are not re-measured across child ages; age-specific rows refer to the child outcome panels linked to those baseline pregnancy measures.

title: Clinical characteristics
section: 3 Sample counts by visit and intervention

title: Clinical characteristics
Clinical characteristics
text:
5 Categorical variables by visit


Show code
categorical_summary <- function(df, vars, group_var = "Intervention") {
  vars <- intersect(vars, names(df))
  if (length(vars) == 0) return(data.frame())

  cat_vars <- vars[vapply(df[vars], function(x) {
    x_chr <- as.character(x)
    n_non_na <- sum(!is.na(x_chr) & nzchar(x_chr))
    n_non_na >= 20L && length(unique(x_chr[!is.na(x_chr) & nzchar(x_chr)])) <= 10L
  }, logical(1))]

  if (length(cat_vars) == 0) return(data.frame())

  bind_rows(lapply(cat_vars, function(vn) {
    xv <- as.character(df[[vn]])
    xv[is.na(xv) | xv == ""] <- "<missing>"

    overall <- as.data.frame(table(xv), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
      transmute(
        variable = pretty_var(vn),
        variable_code = vn,
        group = "Overall",
        level = xv,
        n = Freq,
        pct = 100 * Freq / sum(Freq)
      )

    by_group <- data.frame()
    if (group_var %in% names(df)) {
      g <- as.character(df[[group_var]])
      g[is.na(g) | g == ""] <- "<missing_group>"
      by_group <- as.data.frame(table(g, xv), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
        group_by(g) %>%
        mutate(pct = 100 * Freq / sum(Freq)) %>%
        ungroup() %>%
        transmute(
          variable = pretty_var(vn),
          variable_code = vn,
          group = g,
          level = xv,
          n = Freq,
          pct = pct
        )
    }

    bind_rows(overall, by_group)
  }))
}

cat_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(visits, function(v) {
  vn <- paste0("visit_", v)
  if (!vn %in% names(experiments(mae))) return(NULL)
  cd <- as.data.frame(SummarizedExperiment::colData(experiments(mae)[[vn]]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  vars <- unique(c(exposure_map[[as.character(v)]], core_covars, sensitivity_covars))
  tab <- categorical_summary(cd, vars, group_var = "Intervention")
  if (nrow(tab) == 0) return(NULL)
  tab$child_age <- visit_label(v)
  tab
})) %>%
  select(child_age, variable, variable_code, group, level, n, pct)

make_table(round_numeric(cat_tbl, 3), caption = "Categorical clinical and panel-linked exposure summaries by child age")

title: Data quality and assumptions
text:
Show code
source("analysis_data_utils.R")
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

visits <- c(4L, 5L, 6L, 7L)

analysis_obj <- prepare_analysis_data(
  mae_path = "../data_v2/MAE_original.rds",
  visits = paste0("visit_", visits)
)

df <- analysis_obj$data
metabolite_cols <- analysis_obj$metabolite_cols

raw_df <- extract_long_from_mae(
  analysis_obj$mae,
  visits = paste0("visit_", visits)
)

results <- readRDS("../output/results/targeted_v2/husna_study2_results_v2.rds")
model_status <- results$model_status

visit_label <- function(v) dplyr::case_when(
  v == 4L ~ "6 Months",
  v == 5L ~ "12 Months",
  v == 6L ~ "24 Months",
  v == 7L ~ "5-6 Years",
  TRUE ~ paste("Visit", v)
)

pretty_var <- function(x) {
  x <- sub("^M", "", x)
  x <- gsub("DietaryPatterns", "Dietary Patterns ", x)
  x <- gsub("DIINormalDiet", "Dietary Inflammatory Index ", x)
  x <- gsub("DIIDensityDiet", "Dietary Inflammatory Index Density ", x)
  x <- gsub("^IDQ1$", "IDQ", x)
  x <- gsub("^IDQ2$", "IDQ", x)
  x <- gsub("EPercent", "Percent ", x)
  x <- gsub("Fibtot", "Fibre ", x)
  x <- gsub("CHO", "Carbohydrate", x)
  x <- gsub("Pro", "Protein", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn3", "n-3 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn6", "n-6 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("SFA", "SFA", x)
  x <- gsub("MUFA", "MUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("PUFA", "PUFA", x)
  x <- sub("[12]$", "", x)
  x <- gsub("_", " ", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("\\s+", " ", trimws(x))
  tools::toTitleCase(tolower(x))
}

candidate_vars <- c(
  "MDietaryPatterns_1", "MDIINormalDiet1", "MDIIDensityDiet1", "MIDQ1",
  "MEPercentProDiet1", "MEPercentCHODiet1", "MEPercentFatDiet1",
  "MEPercentPUFADiet1", "MEPercentMUFADiet1", "MEPercentSFADiet1",
  "MEPercentFAn3Diet1", "MEPercentFAn6Diet1", "MEPercentFibtotDiet1",
  "MDietaryPatterns_2", "MDIINormalDiet2", "MDIIDensityDiet2", "MIDQ2",
  "MEPercentProDiet2", "MEPercentCHODiet2", "MEPercentFatDiet2",
  "MEPercentPUFADiet2", "MEPercentMUFADiet2", "MEPercentSFADiet2",
  "MEPercentFAn3Diet2", "MEPercentFAn6Diet2", "MEPercentFibtotDiet2",
  "Intervention", "CGender", "MEthnicity", "MDise",
  "MprepBMI", "MAge1", "MprevGDM_new", "Bfdurationm",
  "MPRSmoke", "MUniEdu", "CWeightBirth"
)

missingness_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(visits, function(v) {
  dv <- df %>% filter(visit_num == v)

  bind_rows(lapply(candidate_vars, function(var_nm) {
    miss_fraction <- mean(is.na(dv[[var_nm]]))
    data.frame(
      child_age = visit_label(v),
      variable = pretty_var(var_nm),
      variable_code = var_nm,
      n_missing = sum(is.na(dv[[var_nm]])),
      missing_fraction = miss_fraction,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
})) %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_fraction), child_age, variable)

make_table(round_numeric(missingness_tbl, 3), caption = "Variable missingness by child age")

sparsity_summary <- bind_rows(lapply(visits, function(v) {
  dv <- raw_df %>% filter(visit_num == v)
  mat <- as.matrix(dv[, metabolite_cols, drop = FALSE])
  observed_fraction <- colMeans(!is.na(mat))

  data.frame(
    child_age = visit_label(v),
    n_features = length(observed_fraction),
    median_observed_fraction = median(observed_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    p10_observed_fraction = as.numeric(quantile(observed_fraction, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
    features_obs_lt_0_50 = sum(observed_fraction < 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),
    features_obs_lt_0_70 = sum(observed_fraction < 0.70, na.rm = TRUE),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

make_table(round_numeric(sparsity_summary, 3), caption = "Metabolite observation coverage by child age")

sparse_features <- bind_rows(lapply(visits, function(v) {

title: Data quality and assumptions
text:
4 Model coverage by analysis question


Show code
coverage_tbl <- model_status %>%
  mutate(
    question = dplyr::case_when(
      question == "Q4" ~ "Q1: Early-Pregnancy Age-Specific Models",
      question == "Q5" ~ "Q2: Late-Pregnancy Age-Specific Models",
      question == "Q6" ~ "Q3: Mutual Timing Model",
      question == "Q7" ~ "Q4: Longitudinal Interaction Model",
      TRUE ~ question
    ),
    child_age = ifelse(is.na(visit), NA_character_, visit_label(as.integer(visit))),
    exposure = pretty_var(exposure),
    n_complete = ifelse(
      is.na(n_complete_min) | !is.finite(n_complete_min),
      NA_character_,
      ifelse(n_complete_min == n_complete_max, as.character(n_complete_min), paste0(n_complete_min, "-", n_complete_max))
    )
  ) %>%
  select(question, analysis, child_age, timing, exposure, status, reason, n_complete, n_features_tested, term_family)

make_table(coverage_tbl, caption = "Observed model coverage and execution status from the saved analyses")

title: Data quality and assumptions
text:
5 Log-transform feasibility


Show code
non_positive_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(visits, function(v) {
  dv <- raw_df %>% filter(visit_num == v)
  mat <- as.matrix(dv[, metabolite_cols, drop = FALSE])

  non_positive_fraction <- colMeans(mat <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  has_non_positive <- non_positive_fraction > 0

  data.frame(
    child_age = visit_label(v),
    metabolites_with_non_positive = sum(has_non_positive, na.rm = TRUE),
    fraction_with_non_positive = mean(has_non_positive, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_non_positive_fraction = median(non_positive_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90_non_positive_fraction = as.numeric(quantile(non_positive_fraction, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

make_table(round_numeric(non_positive_tbl, 3), caption = "Non-positive values before log transformation")

title: Data quality and assumptions
text:
6 Sample-level signal quality


Show code
sample_qc <- df %>%
  transmute(
    StudyID,
    child_age = factor(visit_label(visit_num), levels = visit_label(visits)),
    sample_missing_fraction = rowMeans(is.na(across(all_of(metabolite_cols)))),
    median_abs_z = apply(abs(as.matrix(select(., all_of(metabolite_cols)))), 1, median, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

qc_summary <- sample_qc %>%
  group_by(child_age) %>%
  summarise(
    samples = n(),
    median_missing_fraction = median(sample_missing_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90_missing_fraction = as.numeric(quantile(sample_missing_fraction, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
    median_abs_z = median(median_abs_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90_abs_z = as.numeric(quantile(median_abs_z, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

make_table(round_numeric(qc_summary, 3), caption = "Sample-level quality summary")

title: Data quality and assumptions
text:
9 Raw vs transformed distribution check


Show code
sample_values <- function(dat, cols, label, max_points = 50000L) {
  vals <- as.numeric(as.matrix(dat[, cols, drop = FALSE]))
  vals <- vals[is.finite(vals)]
  if (length(vals) > max_points) {
    set.seed(123)
    vals <- sample(vals, max_points)
  }
  data.frame(scale = label, value = vals, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

dist_raw <- sample_values(raw_df, metabolite_cols, "raw")
dist_transformed <- sample_values(df, metabolite_cols, "log10+z")

dist_df <- bind_rows(dist_raw, dist_transformed)

dist_summary <- dist_df %>%
  group_by(scale) %>%
  summarise(
    n_values = n(),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    p01 = as.numeric(quantile(value, 0.01, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
    median = as.numeric(quantile(value, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
    p99 = as.numeric(quantile(value, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

make_table(round_numeric(dist_summary, 3), caption = "Distribution summary before and after transformation")










Show code
plot_limits <- quantile(dist_df$value, probs = c(0.01, 0.99), na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(
  dist_df %>% filter(value >= plot_limits[[1]], value <= plot_limits[[2]]),
  aes(x = value, color = scale, fill = scale)
) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.2) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution check: raw vs log10+z",
    x = "Value (trimmed to 1st-99th percentile)",
    y = "Density",
    color = "Scale",
    fill = "Scale"
  )
